text.title: Unit Testing — Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every unit-testing application must obey.

rule.statement: Each test verifies exactly one observable behaviour. If the test name needs 'and', split it.
rule.rationale: Multi-behaviour tests obscure failure cause; the first failed assert masks the rest.

rule.statement: Arrange, Act, Assert are explicit (whitespace, comments, or order). The Act line is one call.
rule.rationale: AAA structure makes the test's intent self-documenting.

rule.statement: Name tests test_<behaviour>_when_<context>. Avoid test_1, test_user, test_happy_path.
rule.rationale: The name is the spec; CI failures should read like 'X breaks when Y'.

rule.statement: Mock only at process boundaries (network, disk, clock). Do not mock internal classes/functions.
rule.rationale: Internal mocks couple tests to implementation; boundary mocks test the unit's contract.

rule.statement: No randomness, no real time, no real network. Seed RNGs; freeze clocks; mock HTTP.
rule.rationale: Deterministic tests are debuggable and parallel-safe.

rule.statement: When a dependency is used by many tests, write a fake (full minimal in-memory impl) rather than per-test mocks.
rule.rationale: Fakes give realistic behaviour without coupling each test to the dependency's method names.
